title: fimbristylis cymosa subsp. spathacea
intro: Fimbristylis cymosa subsp. spathacea, also known as the coastal hair sedge, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the family Cyperaceae. It has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant has been reported to have several health benefits, such as reducing fever and relieving digestive issues, due to its compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various ailments and promote overall well-being.
